tools.name: compliance_scan_gdpr
tools.description:
Use this when you need to check a website's GDPR compliance. Scans the URL plus /privacy-policy and /cookie-policy pages. Returns a compliance report in JSON.

Returns: 1. complianceScore (0-100) 2. cookieConsent (detected boolean, type) 3. privacyPolicy (found boolean, url) 4. termsOfService (found

tools.name: compliance_scan_gdpr
tools.description:
Use this when you need to check a website's GDPR compliance. Scans the URL plus /privacy-policy and /cookie-policy pages. Returns a compliance report in JSON. POST variant of compliance_scan_gdpr -- same params passed as JSON body instead of query string.

Returns: 1. complianceScore (0-100) 2. cook

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "compliance_scan_gdpr" references sensitive artifacts (cookie). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: compliance_scan_gdpr, compliance_scan_gdpr · Private data: compliance_scan_gdpr, compliance_scan_gdpr · Public sink: compliance_scan_gdpr, compliance_scan_gdpr.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: compliance_scan_gdpr, compliance_scan_gdpr · Private data: compliance_scan_gdpr, compliance_scan_gdpr · Public sink: compliance_scan_gdpr, compliance_scan_gdpr

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "compliance_scan_gdpr" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet:
before: "Use this when you need to check a website's GDPR compliance. Scans the URL plus /privacy-policy and /cookie-policy pages. Returns a compliance report in JSON.

Returns: 1. complianceScore (0-100) 2. cookieConsent (detected boolean, type) 3. privacyPolicy (found boolean, url) 4. termsOfService (found" → after: "Use this when you need to check a website's GDPR compliance. Scans the URL plus /privacy-policy and /cookie-policy pages. Returns a compliance report in JSON. POST variant of compliance_scan_gdpr -- same params passed as JSON body instead of query string.

Returns: 1. complianceScore (0-100) 2. cook"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
